what: Declarative framework for batch and streaming ETL pipelines.
note: Existing DLT code still runs; classic SKUs kept the DLT prefix. Databricks docs increasingly shorthand this to "Lakeflow pipelines"; the open-source engine is "Spark Declarative Pipelines".

what: Orchestration for multi-task jobs and pipelines.
note: Folded into the Lakeflow umbrella alongside Connect and Declarative Pipelines. The sidebar now groups it with pipelines under "Jobs & Pipelines".

what: SQL-optimized compute that powers Databricks SQL.
note: Databricks docs date this to 2023; some write-ups say 2022.

what: In-workspace Git client for version-controlling notebooks and files.
note: Git folders support asset types Repos didn't and require a remote repo URL.

what: In-workspace UI for browsing and managing Unity Catalog securables — schemas, tables, models, functions, and other assets.
note: Renamed to reflect that it manages all Unity Catalog securables, not just data. Shows as "Catalog" in the sidebar.

what: AI-assisted dashboards for visualizing and sharing query results.
note: The user-facing product is "AI/BI Dashboards"; the underlying API still uses the "Lakeview" name. Distinct from the older SQL/Redash "legacy dashboards".

what: Infrastructure-as-code format for defining and deploying Databricks resources (jobs, pipelines, etc.) as a versioned bundle.
note: Docs read "Declarative Automation Bundles (formerly known as Databricks Asset Bundles)"; the DABs acronym was kept. Supersedes the deprecated dbx deployment tool.

what: Curated, governed conversational agents scoped to a business domain, now able to take autonomous multi-step actions.
note: Renamed in early July 2026 to reflect the shift from conversational query spaces to autonomous agents. Shows as "Genie Agents" in the sidebar.

what: Business-user-facing UI giving non-technical users a single conversational entry point to data and AI.
note: Genie now includes all capabilities previously known as Databricks One, plus account-level access and native mobile apps.

what: Managed search over Unity Catalog data; now supports full-text and hybrid search without pre-computed embeddings.
note: Renamed at GA on 2026-06-25 as the service expanded beyond pure vector search. The "Mosaic AI" prefix came from the 2023 MosaicML acquisition branding.
